title: T-045 — Postgres app-role connection split (separate role for app, never superuser in tests)
prompt:
# T-045 — Postgres app-role connection split (separate role for app, never superuser in tests)

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5 (RLS default — testy zawsze z app-role), §13 (Tests run w app-role w CI)
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Split Postgres connections into a privileged role (migrations only — owner) and a non-privileged app role (RLS-enforced runtime). Tests MUST connect via the app role per PRD §5/§13; superuser connections in test code are a CI failure.

## Implementation contract
(1) Add packages/db/migrations/0010_app_role.sql creating role monopilot_app with NOLOGIN-template + a per-env LOGIN role monopilot_app_local that inherits it; GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA public + SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on existing tables; FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on all RLS-enabled tables; revoke superuser-style privileges. (2) Add packages/db/src/clients.ts exporting two connection factories: getOwnerConnection() (uses DATABASE_URL_OWNER, used by drizzle-kit + migrations) and getAppConnection() (uses DATABASE_URL_APP, used at runtime + tests). (3) Update packages/db/src/index.ts to default-export getAppConnection(). (4) Add an ESLint rule (no-restricted-imports) blocking imports of getOwnerConnection from anywhere except packages/db/src/migrations/** and scripts/migrate.ts. (5) Add Vitest asserting that running a SELECT on tenants via app-role with no app.current_org_id set returns 0 rows (RLS active), while owner returns all rows.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/migrations/0010_app_role.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/clients.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/.eslintrc.cjs [create]
- packages/db/src/__tests__/app-role.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs against a fresh DB, when 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate' completes, then role monopilot_app exists with no SUPERUSER, no BYPASSRLS, and FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is applied to tenants/organizations/users
2. Given the app role connects without setting app.current_org_id, when SELECT * FROM tenants runs, then it returns 0 rows; given the owner role connects, when the same SELECT runs, then it returns all rows (RLS bypass via owner)
3. Given a developer attempts to import getOwnerConnection from anywhere outside packages/db/src/migrations/** or scripts/migrate.ts, when 'pnpm lint' runs, then ESLint reports no-restricted-imports and exits non-zero
4. Given the test suite is configured, when 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test' runs, then every test connects via getAppConnection() (asserted by a beforeAll that introspects current_user)

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/src/__tests__/app-role.test.ts asserting all four ACs (with owner connection injected only for assertion #2 and beforeAll introspection) before clients.ts exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration && pnpm lint'

## Risk red lines
- Do not GRANT BYPASSRLS to monopilot_app — that defeats the entire RLS contract from T-007
- Do not allow application code to import getOwnerConnection — migrations only
- Do not skip FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY — without it, the table owner connection silently bypasses RLS

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-002
- T-007

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation), verification-before-completion, and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Postgres role split — owner role for migrations only, app role for runtime + tests with FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; ESLint blocks owner-import outside migrations.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD §5 says 'testy zawsze z app-role (nigdy superuser)' and §13 has it on the niefunkcjonalne checklist. T-007 created RLS policies but the project still uses a single connection. This task locks the split + adds ESLint guardrails so accidental superuser usage fails CI.
out_of_scope: Do not modify files outside the scope_files list
Do not introduce business logic from later module PRDs (01-NPD, 02-SETTINGS, etc.)
No connection-pool tuning (deferred — Phase C ops)
No PgBouncer config (deferred — Phase C ops)
